content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

DEC 3 1990 PM '90

November 30, 1990

Scott Hallabrin
Senior Staff Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

     This letter is sent to confirm telephone advice
you provided me during a series of telephone
conversations regarding the application of the
Proposition 73 contribution limitations. This advice
was sought on behalf of the California Democratic
Party.

     Generally the FPPC staff is advising that the
limited stay of Judge Karlton's ruling in SEIU will be
dissolved when the Supreme Court's decision in
Taxpayers becomes final; that the SEIU decision did not
invalidate the contribution limitations in Proposition
73 applicable to special elections; that Government
Code section 85304 prohibits transfers to a candidate
in a special election, including transfers from that
candidate's other committees; and that the Commission
will be considering these and related issues at its
December 4, 1990 meeting, at which time the Commission
may adopt a different position.

     You also advised me that the contribution
limitations in Government Code section 85302 do not
apply to funds used by a political committee, broad
based political committee or political party to make
contributions to candidates in special elections,
including the January 8, 1991 special senate election.
You indicated that this would include funds used for
membership communications from a political party to its
members at the behest of a candidate in a special
election. However, since the transfer prohibition
applies in special election campaigns, the party should
not use any funds for this purpose which were received
from candidates. In addition, there are no limits on
the Party's expenditures for communications to its
members made at the behest of a candidate and which
advocate the election of the candidate or defeat of the
candidate's opponent.

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE I. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD C. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
November 30, 1990
Page Two

     The above advice regarding special elections is effective
now and is not contingent upon the finality of the decision in
the Taxpayers case.

     Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
PONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]

DIANE M. FISHBURN

DMF:sk

Law Offices of
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

January 9, 1991

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: A-90-733

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

We received the letter dated December 20, 1990 which
was sent in response to my letter of November 30, 1990. My letter was sent to confirm telephone advice provided to this firm on behalf of the California Democratic Party. We have two concerns with the December 20 letter.

First, we asked for confirmation of the advice that the limits in Government Code section 85302 do not apply to funds used by a political party for communications to its members made at the behest of a legislative candidate in a special election and which advocate the election of the candidate or defeat of the candidate's opponent. While this advice was confirmed by your office in two separate previous phone calls, it is not specifically confirmed in the December 20 letter, and we would like written confirmation.

Second, the letter states that we were not advised that the limitations in Government Code section 83302 no longer applied to funds received by a political committee, broad based committee, or political party and used to make contributions to candidates in legislative special elections. This is not the case. We had a series of conversations which culminated in clear telephone advice that these limitations no longer applied with express reference to legislative special elections. The December 20 letter changes this advice. I believe your letter should have reflected this change in advice rather than state unequivocally we were never so advised.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

DIANE M. FISHBURN
DMF:sk
cc: Fair Political Practices Commissioners

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280
